image: epithelioid cell type and prominent nucleoli (arrow).jpg
res:
Microscopic findings: Microscopic description: The lesion is composed of a nodular and infiltrative pattern of spindle cells with a pushing border. The cells have irregular nuclear contours, hyperchromatic nuclei, and scant cytoplasm. There is no significant mitotic activity or necrosis observed. The lesion shows a clear interface with the surrounding stroma.
Key supporting features: nodular; infiltrative; pushing border; hyperchromatic nuclei; scant cytoplasm.
Likely interpretation: Intraocular melanoma; differential includes amelanotic melanoma and other spindle cell tumors. gts:
Microscopic findings: Epithelioid melanoma cells with prominent nucleoli are present.
Pathological diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma

image: low power shows a tumor in the subretinal area.jpg
res:
Microscopic findings: Microscopic description: The image shows a well-demarcated, nodular lesion with a pushing border. The lesion is composed of spindle cells arranged in fascicles and interwoven networks, with some areas showing a storiform pattern. There is minimal stromal fibrosis and no significant necrosis or hemorrhage noted. The nuclei are oval to elongated, with moderate hyperchromasia and scant cytoplasm.
Key supporting features: nodular; fascicular; storiform; pushing border; minimal fibrosis; hyperchromatic nuclei; scant cytoplasm.
Likely interpretation: Fibrous histiocytoma; differential includes benign fibrous histiocytoma and cellular fibrous histiocytoma. Microscopic description: The image depicts a multifocal, infiltrative lesion with a mixed cell population. gts:
Microscopic findings: Low power view shows an intraocular melanoma mass involving the posterior choroid/subretinal region.
Pathological diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma

res:
Microscopic findings: Microscopic description: The image shows a well-demarcated, nodular lesion in the corneal stroma. The lesion has a pushing border and extends into the surrounding corneal stroma without involving the epithelium. The cells are spindle-shaped with moderate nuclear pleomorphism and scant cytoplasm. There is no significant mitotic activity or necrosis observed.
Key supporting features: nodular; pushing border; spindle cells; nuclear pleomorphism; no mitoses; no necrosis.
Likely interpretation: Intraepithelial neoplasm; consider squamous cell carcinoma in situ or other keratinizing lesions. To refine the differential diagnosis, additional information on the presence of keratinization, ulceration, or specific cellular details would be helpful. res:
Microscopic findings: Microscopic description: The lesion is located in the corneal stroma, presenting as a multifocal, infiltrative pattern with irregular nodular and plaque-like areas. The cells are predominantly spindle-shaped with indistinct borders, showing moderate nuclear pleomorphism and hyperchromasia. There is a mixed inflammatory cell infiltrate present, including lymphocytes and plasma cells, with some areas of necrosis and hemorrhage.
Key supporting features: multifocal, infiltrative, nodular, hyperchromatic nuclei, mixed inflammatory cells, necrosis, hemorrhage.
Likely interpretation: Intraepithelial neoplasia or possible corneal intraepithelial neoplasia; differential includes corneal squamous cell carcinoma or other keratinizing lesions.
